mnemonics.title: Selection Sort Steps
mnemonics.content: FIND MINIMUM - SWAP TO FRONT - REPEAT FOR REST (FMS RFR)

mnemonics.title: Bubble Sort Steps
mnemonics.content: COMPARE ADJACENT - SWAP IF WRONG - BUBBLE UP MAX - REPEAT (CAS BMR)

mnemonics.title: Complexity记忆
mnemonics.content: SELECTION: Always Square (n²); BUBBLE: Best is Nice (n), Avg/Worst are Awful (n²)

acronyms.usage: Algorithm that selects minimum from unsorted portion
Algorithm that bubbles up largest elements
Describes how runtime grows with input size n
Best case performance of an algorithm

visualTips.visualization: Think of selecting the smallest card from a shuffled deck and placing it at the top, then repeating with remaining cards.
Imagine air bubbles rising in water - larger air bubbles rise faster to the surface (end of array).
One pass = one full sweep through array; one iteration = one single comparison or swap operation.
Stable = items with equal keys keep their original order; Unstable = their order may change.

analogies.analogy: Like arranging people by height: find the shortest person and put them first, then find the next shortest from remaining, and so on.
Like sorting marbles by pushing the largest ones to the end of a row through repeated pairwise comparisons and exchanges.
If you have 10 items, you make about 100 comparisons - like checking every person against every other person at a party.
Organizing books on a shelf without using another shelf - you only use your hands (constant extra space).
